behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection/encoding plus CLI parsing via subprocess. A mocked diff can miss real `git` rename output forms and index/worktree nuances. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it is about git repo change categorization, with no AI/ML step and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git diff behavior for binary content and subprocess I/O. In-process fakes often do not reproduce binary diff markers/exit-path behavior that causes crashes. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: binary git diffs only, no model output dependence, no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify a binary file, run CLI subprocess on that repo, assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Unborn-HEAD/no-commit repositories produce real git command failures/messages and exit codes; this is a subprocess+repo-state boundary. Mocks can hide the actual failure mode. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: only git CLI/repo state handling, not AI/ML or agent pipeline behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess, assert zero/empty structured report on stdout and no error exit due to missing commit history.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, requiring real git history traversal and subprocess output. Stubbing history can miss real command semantics and parsing mismatches. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: deterministic git history accounting, no AI/ML dependence, no agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with a known sequence (add/delete/rename/modify across commits), run CLI subprocess, and assert each reported category count matches the known ground truth from that real history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/repo-state and subprocess interactions. In-process fakes usually flatten these states and can hide mismatches. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: comparison of git states only, with no LLM/model decision and no multi-agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once unstaged and once staged, run CLI subprocess for both states, and assert normalized stdout reports are identical.
